item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing genuinely made me feel uncomfortable, especially the test log. Well done for all of that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those are some tough containment procedures ;-; Poor guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're probably thinking 2017. But it could just as easily be 2000. Or 2006. Could be 1950, 1961, 1967, 1972, 1978, 1989, or 1995. Or, going forward, 2023, 2028, 2034, or 2045.

Thanksgiving dates do repeat, and not infrequently. The posting date of the scip itself is irrelevant, as that's entirely out-of-universe.
Going by dialog and the mention of a plastic cornucopia, we can assume it's probably mid-20th century to mid-21st or so, but beyond that, you really can't pin it down concretely as easily as you assume.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was one of the more creative skips I read. Well done indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't that big of a deal, but I couldn't help but notice how you gave the day and month of an event, then blocked out the year, but then you proceed to say that the day was Thanksgiving Day. You can easily figure out the year in question. There's not really a point in redacting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this basically a hardcore version of Food Boy? I was laughing from start to finish.

+1

item.title: Hillarious yet horrifying
item.content:encoded: Enough body horror to avoid needing -j reclassification, and I like the idea of Eldrich Goodness trying to help but not really understanding. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bon fucking appetite.

I read this entire log in the voice of John McClain. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
C-cookie cake and beer?

Oh man I could just feel the distress and fear during this statement. This hammered the +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great fucking thanks, I'm skipping dinner.

Oh and +1

item.title: Re: WTF
item.content:encoded: The reaction is still rather demonstrative of a lack of familiarity with a lot of the site's content, and indicates a lack of the maturity needed to deal with shit that ain't up your alley. It's just annoying.

item.title: Re: WTF
item.content:encoded:
I mean, I don't think they have to "get used to it" if they want to, at this point there are enough materials in this site that people could stick on more action-oriented articles, simpler absurdity/mysteries, or even wholesome articles like 999, otamatone, or eyepod.

Not every article is 400 yo

item.title: Re: WTF
item.content:encoded: So? Weirdness and horror are encouraged on this site. There are a lot more graphic and squicky descriptions of unpleasant situations on the SCP wiki, so please get used to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first it was disgusting, then it became hilarious in a gross kind of way.

The testing log made this SCP, especially with how SCP-3307 apparently really hates his job.

item.title: WTF
item.content:encoded:
Why? Why does this exist? Does it need to exist?
This SCP is too weird!!! Please make this less weird!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The testing log made this a lot better then it was before.

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
(Sorry for the semi-necropost staff please don't shoot me)
I really appreciate that you've done this, it's great as an author to see that my work made enough of an impact on someone that they'd want to do something like this! Thanks!

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
So, I read this… Then I made this:

https://youtu.be/x4Fj6oyHR38

I'm so sorry

item.title: Whoa...
item.content:encoded: Whenever there's someone near SCP-3307, he shits and vomits food, and he leaks drinks from his nose or pees them. SCP-3307 went from being just another Foundation agent to being this weirdo, and became a fucking skip!

item.title: Re: Added Test Log
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, I suppose it works in delivering how the SCP works.

As an aside, the Japanese goddess of food (Uke Mochi) is said to have created fish, meat, and rice by spitting them from her mouth.

item.title: Added Test Log
item.content:encoded: Adding a comment to say that I added a testing log, in case anyone who's already/hasn't read this wants to give it a look!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I think I will consider writing an interview (probably later today, after work). I can see a few jokes I could work in with the food. Maybe if one of the D-Classes' favorite food was a Rump Roast. Or a Moon Cake. ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°).
And thanks for the feedback on the Special Containment Procedures, I'll make an edit to that as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disgusting, but I'm with MrWrong in that we could be provided with a stronger visual for the result of this process. I'd recommend cutting down on the second paragraph of the description, and retooling some of that information into a table format, to provide the same level of detail (in its operation) alongside illustrative examples. You may also find a way to sneak in some humor here as well.

Should you go the route of an interview as Roget suggested, you don't need to necessarily need to have some rote explanation of the event leading to his abilities, and if you're considering imparting some of his experience, I'd err away from anything dismal. I would suggest a scene in which a D-class or two are introduced to the agent, and have to put up with testing his foodstuffs. It'd help if the agent was a total jackass about the whole thing, or is otherwise enjoying their disgust.

As-is, its mildly amusing, and definitely unique, but I think you're selling yourself short with just clinical description.

ETA:

During testing, any windows present must be covered, or otherwise made unavailable for SCP-3307 to see through, in order to limit unintended production of materials.

I see what you're getting at with this, but I think a more sensible option would be to simply stick a bag over its head or something of the sort during transport to and from the testing chamber (why would it have windows to begin with?) or otherwise note caution to prevent line-of-sight with personnel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did consider that, but I ultimately decided against it because it just didn't feel relevant. I can't think of anything an interview would have added other than 'Is thing thing?' 'Yes thing is indeed thing'. The agent is just as clueless as to what happened as the researchers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this could be explored more thoroughly but what you've got feels complete at least. I would enjoy seeing more of this agent's experience becoming an SCP and seeing how they feel although I understand not wanting to be too close to the vermin king or other existing personnel turned anomalous article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback. I'll add in a bit about the appearance now, surprised I didn't think to do something like that before.
Edit: Done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there would be more mileage if you actually show what kind of food can be created. Since they are of equal nutrition to normal food, the juxtaposition with their appearance and means of creation would provide a stronger imagery than just the general process itself.

item.title: AssCP
item.content:encoded:
Here goes nothing! This is the story of your run of the mill eldritch abomination. But, unlike what seems to be the common portrayal now, it doesn't really see us as pests to be squashed. It thinks of us more like a person with an ant farm thinks of their ants. So it tries to help us out, and it gives us a gift. Without knowing that we would think that seeing a guy vomit out food for us is freaking disgusting.

Thanks to Petrograd in the forums for helping out with the draft, and psul for helping in chat after posting!
